post.description:
List Administrators

 ListAdministrators returns all administrators and their roles matching the request and the caller's permissions to retrieve.

 Required permissions depend on the resource type:
   - "iam.member.read" for instance administrators
   - "org.member.read" for organization administrators
   - "project.member.read" for project administrators
   - "project.grant.member.read" for project grant administrators
   - no permissions required for listing own administrator roles

Create Administrator

 CreateAdministrator grants an administrator role to a user for a specific resource.

 Note that the roles are specific to the resource type.
 This means that if you want to grant a user the administrator role for an organization and a project,
 you need to create two administrator roles.

 Required permissions depend on the resource type:
   - "iam.member.write" for instance administrators
   - "org.member.write" for organization administrators
   - "project.member.write" for project administrators
   - "project.grant.member.write" for project grant administrators

Update Administrator

 UpdateAdministrator updates the specific administrator role.

 Note that any role previously granted to the user and not present in the request will be revoked.

 Required permissions depend on the resource type:
   - "iam.member.write" for instance administrators
   - "org.member.write" for organization administrators
   - "project.member.write" for project administrators
   - "project.grant.member.write" for project grant administrators

Delete Administrator

 DeleteAdministrator revokes an administrator role from a user.

 In case the administrator role is not found, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.
 You can check the deletion date in the response to verify if the administrator role was deleted during the request.

 Required permissions depend on the resource type:
   - "iam.member.delete" for instance administrators
   - "org.member.delete" for organization administrators
   - "project.member.delete" for project administrators
   - "project.grant.member.delete" for project grant administrators

id.title: id
id.description: Only return resources that belong to this id.

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines the condition (e.g., equals, before, after) that the timestamp of the retrieved resources should match.

instance.title: instance
instance.description: Instance is returned if the administrator roles were granted on the instance level.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Organization provides information about the organization the administrator roles were granted for.

project.title: project
project.description: Project provides information about the project the administrator roles were granted for.

projectGrant.title: project_grant
projectGrant.description: ProjectGrant provides information about the project grant the administrator roles were granted for.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the administrator role was granted.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
ChangeDate is the timestamp when the administrator role was last updated.
 In case the administrator role was not updated, this field is equal to the creation date.

user.title: user
user.description: User is the user who was granted the administrator role.

roles.title: roles
roles.description: Roles are the roles that were granted to the user for the specified resource.

and.title: and
and.description: Combine multiple authorization queries with an AND operation.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: Search for administrator roles by their change date.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: Search for administrator roles by their creation date.

inUserIdsFilter.title: in_user_ids_filter
inUserIdsFilter.description: Search for administrators roles by the IDs of the users who was granted the administrator role.

or.title: or
or.description:
Combine multiple authorization queries with an OR operation.
 For example, to search for authorizations of multiple OrganizationIDs.

resource.title: resource
resource.description: Search for administrators roles granted for a specific resource.

role.title: role
role.description: Search for administrators roles granted with a specific role.

userDisplayName.title: user_display_name
userDisplayName.description: Search for administrators roles by the display name of the user.

userOrganizationId.title: user_organization_id
userOrganizationId.description: Search for administrators roles by the ID of the organization the user is part of.

userPreferredLoginName.title: user_preferred_login_name
userPreferredLoginName.description: Search for administrators roles by the preferred login name of the user.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: UserID is the ID of the user who should be granted the administrator role.

resource.title: resource
resource.description: Resource is the type of the resource the administrator roles should be granted for.

roles.title: roles
roles.description:
Roles are the roles that should be granted to the user for the specified resource.
 Note that roles are currently specific to the resource type.
 This means that if you want to grant a user the administrator role for an organization and a project,
 you need to create two administrator roles.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the administrator role was created.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: UserID is the ID of the user whose administrator roles should be removed.

resource.title: resource
resource.description: Resource is the type of the resource the administrator roles should be removed for.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description:
DeletionDate is the timestamp when the administrator role was deleted.
 Note that the deletion date is only guaranteed to be set if the deletion was successful during the request.
 In case the deletion occurred in a previous request, the deletion date might not be set.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Paginate through the results using a limit, offset and sorting.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Filter the administrator roles to be returned.

pagination.title: pagination
pagination.description: Pagination contains the current sequence and the total result count.

administrators.title: administrators
administrators.description: Administrators contains the list of administrators matching the request.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the organization the user was granted the administrator role for.

name.title: name
name.description: Name is the name of the organization the user was granted the administrator role for.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the project the user was granted the administrator role for.

name.title: name
name.description: Name is the name of the project the user was granted the administrator role for.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project belongs to.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the project grant the user was granted the administrator role for.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the ID of the project the project grant belongs to.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description: ProjectName is the name of the project the project grant belongs to.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project belongs to.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: GrantedOrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project grant belongs to.

instance.title: instance
instance.description: Search for administrators granted on the instance level.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Search for administrators granted on a specific organization.

projectGrant.title: project_grant
projectGrant.description:
Search for administrators granted on a specific project grant
 by providing both the project ID and the organization ID.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: Search for administrators granted on a specific project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project the project grant belongs to.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project was granted to
 and on which the administrator role was granted too.

instance.title: instance
instance.description: Instance is the resource type for granting administrator privileges on the instance level.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: OrganizationID is required to grant administrator privileges for a specific organization.

projectGrant.title: project_grant
projectGrant.description: ProjectGrantID is required to grant administrator privileges for a specific project grant.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is required to grant administrator privileges for a specific project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ProjectID is the unique identifier of the project the project grant belongs to.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
OrganizationID is the unique identifier of the organization the project was granted to
 and on which the administrator role should be granted.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description: Search for administrators by the granted role.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: UserID is the ID of the user whose administrator roles should be updated.

resource.title: resource
resource.description: Resource is the type of the resource the administrator roles should be granted for.

roles.title: roles
roles.description:
Roles are the roles that the user should be granted.
 Note that any role previously granted to the user and not present in the list will be revoked.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp when the administrator role was last updated.

id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the user.

preferredLoginName.title: preferred_login_name
preferredLoginName.description: PreferredLoginName is the preferred login name of the user. This value is unique across the whole instance.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description:
DisplayName is the public display name of the user.
 By default it's the user's first name and last name, their username or their email address.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: The organization the user belong to.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: Search for administrators by the display name of the user.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the display name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all administrator roles of a user with a display name
 containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

preferredLoginName.title: preferred_login_name
preferredLoginName.description: Search for administrators by the preferred login name of the user.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the preferred login name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all administrator roles of a user with a preferred login name
 containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.

tags.name: zitadel.internal_permission.v2.InternalPermissionService
tags.description:
InternalPermissionService provides methods to manage permissions for resource
 and their management in Zitadel itself also known as "administrators".

 If you want to manage permissions and roles within your project or application,
 please use the AuthorizationsService.
